pageSubtitle: Manage RabbitMQ users and their access permissions
noServerDescription: Add a RabbitMQ server connection to manage users and their access permissions.
selectServerPrompt: Please select a RabbitMQ server to manage users.
accessDenied: Access denied. User management is only available to administrators.
title: Users
userPermissions: User will be granted full permissions (.*) on the selected virtual host
permissionsDescription: Regex patterns controlling what the user can do. Default grants full access.
presetReadOnlyDesc: Consume from queues. No publish or config.
presetWriteOnlyDesc: Publish to exchanges. No consume or config.
tagAdminDesc: Cluster-wide management, policy, and user control.
tagPolicymakerDesc: Create and delete cluster policies and parameters.
tagMonitoringDesc: Read cluster-wide metrics and connection state.
tagManagementDesc: Access the management UI for own resources.
tagImpersonatorDesc: Publish messages on behalf of any user.
administratorWarning: Administrator grants cluster-wide root-equivalent access. Use with care.

toast.alreadyExistsDesc: A user named "{{name}}" already exists on this server.
toast.permissionsFailedRolledBack: Permissions failed — user rollback completed. Retry with different permissions.
toast.rollbackFailed: Rollback failed. The user exists without permissions; review manually.

passwordOptionalHint: On AWS Amazon MQ, a password hash is always generated even without a password.
usernameHelp: Lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores recommended.
selectServer: Please select a server to view user details.
cannotModifyConnectionUser: Cannot modify or delete the connection user. This is the account Qarote uses to connect to this server.
createUserDescription: Add a new RabbitMQ user with optional password and role tags.
removePasswordWarning: Removing the password will disconnect services that authenticate using it.
bulkDeleteDescription: This will permanently delete {{count}} users. This action cannot be undone.
bulkDeleteDescription_one: This will permanently delete {{count}} user. This action cannot be undone.
bulkDeleteDescription_other: This will permanently delete {{count}} users. This action cannot be undone.
deleteUserConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete user {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
cannotModifyProtectedUser: This is a protected system user and cannot be modified or deleted.
passwordAwsNote: On AWS Amazon MQ, a password hash is always generated even without a password.
configureRegexpTooltip: Regular expression pattern controlling which resources the user can configure (create/delete queues, exchanges, bindings). Use ".*" for full access or specific patterns like "^myqueue.*" for resources starting with "myqueue".
writeRegexpTooltip: Regular expression pattern controlling which exchanges and routing keys the user can publish messages to. Use ".*" for full publish access or patterns like "^logs.*" for specific routing keys.
readRegexpTooltip: Regular expression pattern controlling which queues the user can consume messages from (read/get operations). Use ".*" for full read access or patterns like "^user_.*" for queues starting with "user_".
clearPermissionsDescription: This will remove all permissions for this user on virtual host {{vhost}}. The user will no longer be able to access resources on this vhost.
deleteUserDescription: Permanently delete this user and revoke all their permissions. This action cannot be undone.
deleteUserDialogDescription: This will permanently delete the user {{name}} and revoke all their permissions. This action cannot be undone.
regexHint: .* = full access · ^$ = deny all · ^amq\. = default exchanges
